summary: Ukrainian drones are increasingly striking rear areas at distances of 40+ km, with loitering munitions now appearing up to 100 km from the front line, indicating significant improvements in UAV range and payload capacity. Operators are combining cheap, mass-produced FPV drones with more expensive foreign models, rapidly scaling up attacks on both static and moving targets. On the ground, FPV drones continue to be used effectively against Russian assaults, including targeting soldiers on motorcycles and ambushers attempting to feign surrender. Ukrainian forces are also countering Russian electronic warfare by using drone detectors and meme-style EW interference tactics to disrupt enemy FPV operations.
